title: Halcyon Genomics to Buy Gene-Editing Startup Oryza Therapeutics in $1.8B Deal
body:
Halcyon Genomics announced Sunday it has agreed to acquire Oryza Therapeutics, a San Diego-based company developing CRISPR-based treatments for rare metabolic disorders, in a transaction valued at approximately $1.8 billion. The deal, which includes $1.4 billion in cash and the rest in Halcyon stock, is expected to close by the end of the second quarter pending regulatory review.

Oryza's lead program targets Maple Syrup Urine Disease, an inherited condition affecting fewer than 3,000 patients in the United States. The company's platform has shown early promise in animal models, though no human trials have been completed.

Halcyon chief executive Dominic Ferrara said in a statement that the acquisition represents the company's strategic pivot toward rare disease. "We've been looking for the right gene-editing partner for several years," he said. "Oryza's science is genuinely differentiated."

Oryza was founded in 2019 by Dr. Lena Vasquez, who will join Halcyon as head of rare disease research. The startup had raised $340 million across three venture rounds, most recently at a $900 million valuation in late 2024.

Analysts noted that Halcyon is paying a significant premium. Shares of Halcyon fell about 4 percent in pre-market trading Monday.
